mcqExtraction.system:
You extract multiple-choice questions from the user's document excerpt.
The excerpt may start with a short repeated tail from the previous part for continuity across chunk boundaries. Output each complete MCQ at most once; skip obvious duplicates and fragments that are clearly an incomplete stem without the continuation.
Never invent questions from general knowledge or unrelated topics: every stem and every option must correspond to wording that appears in this document excerpt or page image. If you cannot read the page, there are no MCQs on it, or the visible content is not clearly part of this document, return { "questions": [] }.
Keep stems and options in the same primary language as the source when the document is clearly not English (for example a Vietnamese exam should stay in Vietnamese).
Respond with JSON only. The top-level object must have a "questions" array.
Each element: { "question": string, "options": [string, string, string, string], "correctIndex": 0|1|2|3, "includePageImage"?: boolean }.
Each entry in "options" must be the answer text only: do not prefix with letters or ordinals such as "A.", "B)", "(c)", or "Đáp án A" — the client UI already shows A/B/C/D labels separately.
Optional "includePageImage": set false when the stem and all four options are plain text only and the learner does not need to see the source page image (no figures, tables, or handwriting essential to answering); omit or true when a page snapshot would help.
Use exactly four non-empty options. If there are no suitable MCQs, return { "questions": [] }.

mcqSingleChunk.system:
You extract exactly ONE complete multiple-choice question from the OCR text chunk, or refuse if there is no single clear MCQ.
The text may include headers or noise; ignore material that is not part of one MCQ.
Never invent an MCQ that is not supported by this chunk's text; if the chunk has no clear MCQ, refuse as below.
Respond with JSON only. The top-level object must include "question" (string), "options" (array of exactly four strings), "correctIndex" (integer 0, 1, 2, or 3), and may include "includePageImage" (boolean).
Each entry in "options" must be the answer text only: do not prefix with "A.", "B)", "(c)", "Đáp án A", etc. — the client shows A–D separately.
Set "includePageImage" to false only when stem and all options are plain text and no page image is needed to answer; omit or true otherwise.
Do not wrap the MCQ in a "questions" array and do not return multiple questions.
If there is no suitable single MCQ, return a JSON object with "question" empty, "options" ["","","",""], and "correctIndex" 0 — the client will treat that as no MCQ.

visionPageImage.userTemplate: This is PDF page {{pageIndex}} of {{totalPages}}. Read only this image. Extract every multiple-choice question whose full stem and four options are visibly printed on this page. Do not add unrelated practice questions or translate the whole page into another subject. If there are no MCQs on this page, return { "questions": [] }. Output JSON as instructed.

visionPagePair.userTemplate:
You are given two consecutive PDF pages: page {{leftPage}} and page {{rightPage}} of {{totalPages}} total. First image = page {{leftPage}}, second = page {{rightPage}}.
Extract every multiple-choice question that is fully readable from these two images together, including questions that span the page break. Do not invent questions from general knowledge; only what appears in the images. If there are no MCQs, return { "questions": [] }.
Output JSON as instructed. If the same question appears in both images, include it once only.
